data.title: WillScot reduces borrowing costs and extends $3B credit facility to 2030
data.description:
• WillScot Holdings (NASDAQ:WSC) announced the amendment of its asset-based lending (ABL) credit facility, lowering borrowing costs and extending the maturity to October 16, 2030.
  • The amended ABL revolver reduces the facility size from $3.7B to $3B, cutting undrawn line fees, while the accordion feature expands from $750M to $1B.
  • Interest rate spreads over Term SOFR and Term CORRA were reduced from 160 bps to 137.5 bps, with potential step-downs to 112.5 bps based on performance metrics.
  • At current borrowing levels, the company expects to save ~$5M annually in interest expense, with potential for further reductions.
  • The amendment enhances liquidity, with $1.4B in borrowing capacity available as of the transaction close.

data.title: Sphere 3D secures $4M from warrant exercise, issues new warrants in private placement
data.description:
• Sphere 3D (NASDAQ:ANY) entered into a warrant inducement agreement with an institutional investor for the immediate exercise of 4.37M existing warrants at a reduced price of $0.94, generating ~$4M in gross proceeds.
  • The funds will be used for working capital and general corporate purposes.
  • In exchange, the investor will receive 8.74M new unregistered warrants (October 2025 series) in a private placement.
  • The new warrants will also have a $0.94 exercise price, become exercisable after stockholder approval, and will be valid for five years from the approval date.
  • The transaction is expected to close on or about October 17, 2025.
  • ANY shares down 9.3% premarket on Friday.

data.title: Iconic announces equity financing up to $2.55M
data.description:
• Iconic Minerals (ICM:CA) on Friday announced the negotiation of a private placement for gross proceeds of up to $2.55M. 
  • The funds will be raised by the company issuing up to 30M units, at a price of $0.085 per unit, each unit consisting of one common share and one-half share purchase warrant, each whole warrant, entitling the holder thereof to purchase one additional common share, exercisable for a period of two years from the date of issuance at a price of $0.17 per share.
  • Proceeds raised will be used toward expenditures required for drilling and advancement of its exploration program on its New Pass gold property in Nevada, and for general working capital purposes.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: IonQ stockholders plan to sell 6.65M common shares
data.description:
Quantum computing company IonQ (NYSE:IONQ) said certain stockholders are planning to sell a total of about 6.65M common shares of the company.

The company filed a document with the Securities and Exchange Commission on Oct. 16.

data.title: Crypto stocks trade lower amid bitcoin liquidations, broader market sell-offs - report
data.description:
Crypto-linked stocks were trading lower pre-market amid continued bitcoin liquidations and a sell-off in the broader markets, Bloomberg News reported on Friday.

Pre-market decliners include Strategy (MSTR) (-1.65% to -1.71%), Circle Internet (CRCL) (-1.33% to $126.75), Bullish (BLSH) (-3.02% to $55.81), Riot Platforms (RIOT) (-4.04% to $18.76), MARA Holdings (MARA) (-1.80% to $19.90), Coinbase Global (COIN) (-2.04% to $323.50), Bit Digital (BTBT) (-2.98% to $3.58), CleanSpark (CLSK) (-6.60% to $18.67), Hut 8 (HUT) (-4.47% to $46.60), HIVE Digital Technologies (HIVE) (-14.90% to $4.97), IREN (IREN) (-4.59% to $58.99)

Bitcoin liquidations have hit ~$80M in the last twenty four hours, according to data by CoinCentral.

BTC-USD was trading 2.18% lower at $105,865.25 Friday pre-market.

Meanwhile, Wall Street's major averages face increased volatility due to the ongoing U.S.-China trade conflict and bad loan reports in banks, while investors continued to assess earnings reports and the ongoing U.S. government shutdown.

"Market sentiment saw a sharp deterioration over the past 24 hours as news of bad loans at two U.S. regional lenders triggered broader concerns about credit quality, leading U.S. bank stocks to their worst day since early April while the S&P 500 sank -0.63%," Deutsche Bank's Peter Sidorov said.
